---
title: "transaction_analysis_presentation"
format: pptx
---

## Data Analysis Report: Transactional Insights

- Data Analysis Report: Transactional Insights
- Comparison of Cluster Control and Sample 500 Datasets

## Overview of Datasets

- Overview of Datasets
- • Two datasets analyzed: 'cluster_control.csv' (100 rows) and 'sample_500.csv' (500 rows).
- • Shared Schema: transaction_id, customer_id, purchase_amount, product_category, purchase_date.
- • Data Quality: Both datasets are complete with zero missing values.

## Comparative Statistics: Purchase Amount

- Comparative Statistics: Purchase Amount
- • Mean Purchase:
-   - Cluster Control: 89.23
-   - Sample 500: 98.11
- • Range & Dispersion:
-   - Cluster Control: Min 1.11, Max 314.41
-   - Sample 500: Min 1.62, Max 430.91
- • Key Insight: The larger sample (500) captures higher-value transactions and shows greater variance.

## Key Findings & Conclusions

- Key Findings & Conclusions
- • No significant correlation found between transaction IDs, customer IDs, and purchase amounts.
- • The distribution of purchase amounts is relatively stable across both datasets.
- • The 'sample_500' dataset provides a more comprehensive view of extreme high-value transactions.
- • Recommendation: Use the larger sample for modeling extreme value scenarios.
